codeReview.summary: The forwarding-policy changes correctly mark `turn/started`, `turn/completed`, and `serverRequest/*` as always-forwarded while keeping `agentMessage` attach-only, and the new websocket integration tests cover detached forwarding during normal runtime. However, the wrapper still aborts the relay client as soon as the child exits, before the stdout forwarding task has drained buffered output, so a final lifecycle or approval frame can still be lost during shutdown.

issues.description: After `child.wait()` returns, the wrapper tears down the relay path (`drop(relay_tx); relay_handle.abort();`) before it joins `stdout_handle`. If codex writes a final `turn/completed` or `serverRequest/*` message right before exiting, that line can still be buffered in stdout; `stdout_handle` will read it after the relay receiver is gone, the send will fail, and the server will miss the completion/input-required update this feature is supposed to guarantee.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture docs still do not document the forwarding-policy split that now matters for detached notifications: lifecycle and approval events are sent upstream even while detached, while agent text remains attach-only.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:107-111 only says classified messages go to the relay and attached users receive bot forwarding, while mission.md:28 requires lightweight notifications even when not attached; the implemented rule now lives in wrapper/src/proxy.rs:364-367 and wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:51-60.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill still treats separate manual websocket verification as the required procedure, but this worker justifiably relied on the existing real-socket integration tests and reported that deviation in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md:41-45 requires manual verification; /home/dl/.factory/missions/33afaa22-b81b-48d3-b9b3-131d99d630c7/handoffs/2026-03-31T21-31-18-827Z__fix-wrapper-always-forward-lifecycle__aeceafe8-45f7-46a0-9025-d41fdb06a2c8.json:62-71 records `followedProcedure: false` because the worker used integration-style websocket proxy tests instead of a separate manual client.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, architecture/services guidance, the rust-worker skill, the worker transcript skeleton, and commit 3560dc5d91cf72760529fc27ffde2e0e80961651. The feature correctly adds detached lifecycle/serverRequest forwarding, but a shutdown-ordering bug in wrapper/src/proxy.rs can still drop the last lifecycle event on process exit, so the server is not guaranteed to observe every detached turn completion or approval request.
